highlights.text: It is also why I wrote last Wednesday at 4pm: “Bear Case Thursday: Begins on the failure of 6819”.

I wrote on Friday at 4pm: “My general lean is ES can try and recover 6689 to generate a rally to 6743, 6754, 6766, then 6819” We got started right on this after 6pm Sunday and by 7pm we recovered 6689 and ran to 6766+ Today was FOMC day and these are largely coin tosses, but I was looking for bulls to finish the job and backtest that 6815-19 zone.
This are basically coin tosses so I can only give my lean as if it were a normal day.
On a normal day my lean is that on Sunday after the open ES put in a monster Failed Breakdown when we flushed the 6689 shelf of lows that we set last Friday, recovered, and ripped.
My lean is this can keep going.
Last Thursday morning, price picked its path, and down we went going elevator down .
Every elevator down sell sees an equally violent squeeze the other way.
Those squeezes however, only occur when price triggers my core setup: The Failed Breakdown which occurs when price flushes a major low, traps shorts, then reverses.
1) To start a squeeze, they’d need to put in a Failed Breakdown and to do this, theyd need to recover 6689 which was a big shelf all day Friday.
In today’s newsletter I’ll talk this, I’ll go over today’s Failed Breakdowns (these are key to know), and I’ll discuss the actionable plan for tomorrow
Bulls want to hold 6770/64 (or quick trap below) then head to 6785, 6802, 6815-19.

post_title: SPX Sells After FOMC, But Holds The Weeks Lows. Will The FOMC Dip Get Bought? March 19 Plan
